info.title: Unqork Services
info.description: This documents the Unqork Services that are available for use in the Plug-In Component. ## API Access Notes #### Express Module and Workflow Access Express Module and Workflow Access is determined by a User's Role and the Module's permissions. An Express User's Role is specified at the environment level, but can be overwritten at the Application level using Application Roles. If Module Permissions are used, the permission settings will specify what access (Read, Write, Obfuscate, or None) a User will have to the Module. Anonymous Users may also be able to access a Module if the permission settings allow it. #### Submission Access Submission Access is determined by a User's Role, Groups, and if they are the owner of the Submission. A Submission owner is the user that created or updated the Submission. If a user is a Submission owner, Designer Administrator, or an Express Super User, the user has access to the Submission. If the User does not have access to the Module or Workflow that the Submission is associated with, then the User will not have access to the Submission. See Express Module and Workflow Access (Express Module and Workflow Access). A User's Role Groups can also provide a User Access to a Submission. A User needs to have Intersecting Groups with the Submission Owner. Intersecting Groups means a User has a Role with a Group (Groups assigned directly to the User do not count) that is in the Submission owner's groups (the Submission owners Role Groups or the Submission owners User Groups). If a User has Intersecting Groups: - And the Group type is ignore role, a User that has Intersecting Groups can access the Submission. - And the Group type is Role descendents, a User that has Intersecting Groups and the Submission owner's Role is a descendant of the User's Role then the User can access the Submission. - And the Group type is own Role and descendents, a User that has Intersecting Groups and the Submission owner's Role is a descendent of the User's Role or the User's Role is the same as the Submission owner's Role then the User can access the Submission.

get.description:
Queries the configuration analysis results and returns details of most recently run checks
### Authorization Required:
  - Application View

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Format to return config analysis results [json|xlsx] (default json)

parameters.name: appId
parameters.description: Limit to modules of a specific application ID (default all)

parameters.name: moduleId
parameters.description: Limit to module ID or comma-seperated module IDs (default all)

parameters.name: checkType
parameters.description: Check type - for example MODULE (default all)

parameters.name: checkName
parameters.description: Check name or comma-seperated list of check names - checkType is required if checkName is specified (default all)

parameters.name: resultType
parameters.description: Result type [PASS|FAIL|..] (default all)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: default 50 for JSON format, no default for xlsx

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: default 0

schema.description: Array of config analysis results

type.description: type of check, e.g. MODULE, COMPONENT

component.description: component ID, if the check is of type COMPONENT

created.description: When the check run was created

modified.description: When the check run was modified

start.description: When the check run was started (unix timestamp)

end.description: When the check run was started (unix timestamp)

userId.description: user ID of the user who initiated the check run

type.description: PASS|FAIL

get.description:
Queries a Reference Data table and returns the results of the query. The query string should be used to specify fields, filter conditions, and sorting, according to the [api-query-params](https://github.com/loris/api-query-params) library. For example, `/fbu/uapi/query/people/all?age=33` will result in `[{"name":"Alice","age":33},{"name":"Bob","age":33}]`. NOTE: If no filter condition is passed, the default `limit` is 100 (this can be overridden by passing the `limit` parameter). If a filter condition is passed, all results will be returned (unless a limit is passed).
### Authorization Required:
  - Data Collections View

parameters.name: table
parameters.description: Name of the Reference Data table

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return (default 100 if no filter condition is passed, default all if a filter condition is passed, no maximum)

schema.description: Array of Reference Data table rows

get.summary: Get Distinct Values from Reference Data
get.description:
Queries a Reference Data table and returns the distinct list of values from a single field. The query string should be used to specify fields, filter conditions, and sorting, according to the [api-query-params](https://github.com/loris/api-query-params) library. For example, `/fbu/uapi/query/people/distinct?distinct=name&age=33` will result in `[{"name":"Alice"},{"name":"Bob"}]`.
### Authorization Required:
  - Data Collections View

parameters.name: table
parameters.description: Name of the Reference Data table

parameters.name: distinct
parameters.description: Name of the field from which to compute the distinct list of values

schema.description: Array of objects containing a single distinct value

parameters.name: moduleId
parameters.description:
Either moduleId or workflowId are required. Provide only one of them and not both.

Array of Module IDs for which to retrieve submissions.

Example `moduleId[]=5d8531e5ae40bf002d76c9d0&moduleId[]=5d85436917205e002d1f390d`

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description:
Either workflowId or moduleId are required. Provide only one of them and not both.

Array of Workflow IDs for which to retrieve submissions.

Example `workflowId[]=5d8531e5ae40bf002d76c9d0&workflowId[]=5d85436917205e002d1f390d`

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of submission data fields to include in the result. If unspecified, no submission data fields will be included. To include a field from the top level of the submission use \_\_submission\_\_.<top_level_field>. Including top level fields is only available for admins for the _workflow and status objects.

parameters.name: countOnly
parameters.description: Whether to respond with the count of submissions and not the actual submissions

parameters.name: submissionId
parameters.description: Array of submission IDs to include in the result. Example `submissionId[]=5d8531e5ae40bf002d76c9d0&submissionId[]=5d85436917205e002d1f390d`

parameters.name: metadataFields
parameters.description: Comma-separated string of metadata fields to include in the result. If unspecified, no submission metadata fields will be included.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Submission data sort criteria, specified according to the [api-query-params](https://github.com/loris/api-query-params) library.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of submissions to return (e.g. for pagination); by default, all submissions are returned

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: Number of submissions to skip (e.g. for pagination); by default, no submissions are skipped

parameters.name: geoNear
parameters.description: Stringified [$geoNear](https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/operator/aggregation/geoNear) object (geospatial index required). For example: `/fbu/uapi/system/getSubmissions?moduleId=<moduleId>&data.age=33&geoNear={"near":{"type":"Point","coordinates":[-73.99279,40.719296]},"distanceField":"dist"}`

parameters.name: includeDeleted
parameters.description: Whether to include deleted objects in response

schema.description: Array of submission objects containing the requested data/metadata fields

put.description:
Update relationship
### Authorization Required:
  - If elementType = ‘application’ then Application Update to the Workspace associated with the Source and Parent.
  - If actionType = ‘link’ or ‘unlink’ and elementType = ‘module’ then Application Update to the Workspace associated with the Source and Parent.
  - If actionType = ‘link’ or ‘unlink’ and elementType = ‘referenceDataCollection’ then Data Collections Update to the Workspace associated with the Source and Parent.
  - If actionType = ‘transfer’ and elementType = ‘module’ and transferring to an Application within the same Workspace, then Application Update to the Workspace associated with the Source and Parent.
  - If actionType = ‘transfer’ and elementType = ‘referenceDataCollection’ and transferring to an Application within the same Workspace, then Data Collections Update to the Workspace associated with the Source and Parent.
  - If actionType = ‘transfer’ and elementType = ‘module’ or ‘referenceDataCollection’ and transferring to an Application outside of the Workspace, then Designer Administrator or Can Manage Workspace Role to the Workspace associated with the Source and Parent.

post.summary: Generates an encrypted referstring for authentication
post.description:
This endpoint will generate encrypted refer strings that can be used to authenticate users into an Unqork environment

 Environment Variables - The following Environment Variables are required
 - key : key for encryption
 - cipher : method of encryption. The default and recommended cipher is aes-256-gcm.

 Include this refer string in the link to an Unqork resource as follows
   ```
   <hostname>?refer=<referString>/#/display/<resource_id>
   ```
   - hostname - host name of Unqork server
   - referString - referString returned from API
   - resource_id - Id of the Unqork resource to viewFor example, https://client.unqork.io?refer=<referString>/#/display/abcd123

 Examples

   Example 1: Generate Refer String for 1 day (default role)

   Request

   ```
    {
      "userId": "user123",
      "expireOffset": 1,
      "expireMeasure": "days"
    }
   ```

   Response

   ```
    {
      "referString": "<encrypted refer string>"
    }
   ```
   Example 2: Generate Refer String for single use (recommended)

   Request

   ```
    {
      "userId": "user123",
      "expireOffset": 1,
      "expireMeasure": "days",
      "oneTimeUse": true
    }
   ```

   Response

   ```
    {
      "referString": "<encrypted refer string>"
    }
   ```

   Example 3: Generate Refer String for custom role

   Request
  ```
    {
      "userId": "user123",
      "expireOffset": 1,
      "expireMeasure": "days",
      "additionalParams": {
        "role": "customRole"
      }
    }
  ```

   Response
  ```
    {
      "referString": "<encrypted refer string>"
    }
  ```

   Example 4: Generate Refer String with custom user parameters

   Request
  ```
    {
      "userId": "user123",
      "expireOffset": 1,
      "expireMeasure": "days",
      "additionalParams": {
        "custom1": "custom value 1",
        "custom1": "custom value 2",
        "custom1": "custom value 3"
      }
    }
  ```

   Response
  ```
    {
      "referString": "<encrypted refer string>"
    }
  ```
### Authorization Required:
  - Designer Administrator

referString.description: The encrypted refer string containing user information

get.description:
Returns module submission objects for a given module. This is a paged endpoint (see Paging). Module submission data is transformed and returned in a specific format, based on the specified transform. JSON submission data is returned as an object, XML data is returned as a string, and PDF data is returned as a [Cloud Storage Delivery URL](#section/Cloud-Storage-Delivery) to the rendered PDF (and optionally base 64 data).
### Authorization Required:
  - See Submission Access ([Submission Access](#submission-access))

parameters.name: transformName
parameters.description: Transform to apply to module for output. Transform must be configured and designated for for output. Available transforms may be listed via the /transforms endpoint

parameters.name: includeRaw
parameters.description: Whether to include the untransformed raw submission data in addition to the transformed data. Raw submission data may contain [Cloud Storage Delivery URLs](#section/Cloud-Storage-Delivery).

parameters.name: includeBase64
parameters.description: Whether to include base64 PDF data in addition to the PDF url (for "njk-pdf" transforms)

parameters.name: resolveCloudStorageUrls
parameters.description: When `transformName` is specified, Cloud Storage URLs are already resolved to the original base64 value. When this flag is specified, resolve Cloud Storage URLs to base64 data inside "rawData", as well.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Filter conditions against submissions. Currently supported filter conditions are `userId`, `created`, and `modified`. The filters should be `;` separated, as shown below. NOTE: When filtering on `created` and `modified`, all timestamps are in UTC.

Examples:
  - `filter=userId=john@doe.com` will fetch all submissions owned by "john@doe.com"
  - `filter=userId=john@doe.com;created>2019-06-11T21:50:57.067Z` will fetch all submissions owned by "john@doe.com" and created after "2019-06-11T21:50:57.067Z" (UTC)
  - `filter=modified=2019-06-11T21:50:57.067Z` will fetch submissions modified at exactly "2019-06-11T21:50:57.067Z" (UTC)
  - `filter=created>2019-06-11T00:00:00.000Z;created<2019-06-20T00:00:00.000Z` will fetch submissions created between "2019-06-11T00:00:00.000Z" (UTC) and "2019-06-20T00:00:00.000Z" (UTC)


Supported operators (as specified in this library [api-query-params](https://github.com/loris/api-query-params)):
  - key=val  `type=public`
  - key>val  `count>5`
  - key>=val  `rating>=9.5`
  - key<val  `createdAt<2016-01-01`
  - key<=val  `score<=-5`
  - key!=val  `status!=success`
  - key=val1,val2  `country=GB,US`
  - key!=val1,val2  `lang!=fr,en`
  - key  `phone`
  - !key  `!email`
  - key=/value/<opts>  `email=/@gmail\.com$/i`
  - key!=/value/<opts> `phone!=/^06/`

Note: multiple forward slashes (/) are interpreted as a regex. To use a string comparison wrap your parameter with string(). Ex. email=string(/@gmail\.com$/i).

put.description:
Updates multiple module submissions. This operation supports partial "data" updates, i.e. data that is sent in "data" may include some but not all of the submission data. Partial metadata updates are supported without including the entire metadata object. To increment a numeric data key (will also initialize), use "incrementData" To delete a data key, use "unsetData". To delete a metadata key, use "unsetMetadata". To replace the entire data object, use the "replaceData" flag. Module submission data must be provided as a JSON object. Max Limit per request is 50.
### Authorization Required:
  - See Submission Access ([Submission Access](#submission-access))

parameters.name: replaceData
parameters.description: Whether to completely replace the submission data with the object passed in "data". This option is available to "Administrator" users only.

delete.description:

### Authorization Required:
  - See Submission Access ([Submission Access](#submission-access))

parameters.description: Whether to hard delete the submission from database. Value of 'destroy=true' will delete the submission. This option requires administrator privileges. Once deleted, the submission cannot be retrieved.

post.description:
Requires `enable-data-models-feature` feature flag. Validate data against a json schema, either by schema definition or schema ID. `jsonSchema` or `schemaId` are required parameters.
### Authorization Required:
  - Authenticated

parameters.name: validateRequiredFields
parameters.description: If false, ignore required fields of a schema and validate the format only

data.description: Data to validate against

draftVersion.description: JSON Schema draft version. For more information on JSON Schema draft versions - https://json-schema.org/specification-links

fieldMapping.description:
Field map to validate against

e.g. `[{ field: 'name', model: 'modelId', type: 'string', connectedTo: 'name1' }, { field: 'price', model: 'modelId', type: 'number', connectedTo: 'price1' }]`

connectedTo.description: Property ID of the component that is mapped to the data property

field.description: Property ID of the data property

model.description: ID of the data model the field belongs to

type.description: Type of the data property

schema.description:
An object with a key of `data`, with a value that is an object. That object has keys that are the `fieldMapping` `field` with the matching data property as the value. If no `fieldMapping` is passed, then the passed `data` is returned if no errors are encountered.
e.g. Given the data below plus the `fieldMapping` example -

**data:** `{ name1: 'shirt', price1: 1, addProp: 'extra' }`

**jsonSchema:** `{ title: 'Sample Schema', type: 'object', properties: { name: { type: 'string' }, price: { type: 'number' } }, required: ['name'] }`

**Response:**

`{ data: { 'modelId': { name: 'shirt', price: 1 } } }`

NOTE: MongoDB selectors that can be nested inside queries
(such as [Comparison](https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/operator/query/#comparison) and [Element](https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/operator/query/#element) Selectors,
e.g. `$eq`, `$in`, `$exists`) are supported.
The following [Logical](https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/operator/query/#logical) Selectors are also supported: `$and`, `$or`, `$nor`

DataFilter.description:
Filter condition against submission "data" field. Value must be a MongoDB query condition (see Examples, below).

NOTE: MongoDB selectors that can be nested inside queries (such as [Comparison](https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/operator/query/#comparison) and [Element](https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/operator/query/#element) Selectors, e.g. `$eq`, `$in`, `$exists`) are supported. The following [Logical](https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/operator/query/#logical) Selectors are also supported: `$and`, `$or`, `$nor`

Examples:
- `dataFilter: {"age": 30}`
- `dataFilter: {"age": {"$gt": 30}, "people.1.gender": "male"}`

  - Will match submission where age > 30 and gender for the 2nd people element equals "male"

MetadataFilter.description:
Filter condition against submission "metadata" field. Value must be a MongoDB query condition (see Examples, below). MongoDB [dot notation](https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/core/document/#dot-notation) is supported for keys.

The following [Logical](https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/operator/query/#logical) Selectors are also supported: `$and`, `$or`, `$nor`

Examples:
- `metadataFilter: {"checkpoints.complete": 1234}`
- `metadataFilter: {"checkpoints.complete": {"$exists": true}, "checkpoints.processed": {"$exists": false}}`

  - Will return submissions where both `metadata.checkpoints.complete` exists AND `metadata.checkpoints.processed` does not exist

MetadataFilterString.description:
Filter condition against submission "metadata" field. Value must be a stringified MongoDB query condition (see Examples, below). MongoDB [dot notation](https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/core/document/#dot-notation) is supported for keys.

NOTE: MongoDB selectors that can be nested inside queries (such as [Comparison](https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/operator/query/#comparison) and [Element](https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/operator/query/#element) Selectors, e.g. `$eq`, `$in`, `$exists`) are supported. The following [Logical](https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/operator/query/#logical) Selectors are also supported: `$and`, `$or`, `$nor`

Examples:
- `metadataFilter: {"checkpoints.complete": 1234}`
- `metadataFilter: {"checkpoints.complete": {"$exists": true}, "checkpoints.processed": {"$exists": false}}`

  - Will return submissions where both `metadata.checkpoints.complete` exists AND `metadata.checkpoints.processed` does not exist

DataFields.name: dataFields
DataFields.description: Comma-separated list of dot-notation `data` fields to retrieve; for example, `&dataFields=field1,also.field2` will retrieve `{"data":{"field1":"v1","also":{"field2":"v2"}}}`. Do not include "data" in front of each field. All other submission fields will always be retrieved.

OAuth2.description:
The Unqork API implements the [OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials Grant](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-1.3.4) and the [OAuth 2.0 Password Grant](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749#section-1.3.3). Access via OAuth2 Client Credentials Grant can be utilized by creating Client Credentials through the API Access Management Administration page. Access via OAuth2 Password Grant can be enabled for all users in Environment Administration. Once OAuth2 Password Grant is enabled, all Unqork users can use their Unqork username/password to retrieve an access token.

In order to utilize any of the API resources, you must first retrieve an access token by POSTing your credentials to the access token URL, e.g. using `curl`:
```
  $ curl -u '{clientId}:{clientSecret}' -X POST --basic https://xyzfinancial.unqork.io/api/1.0/oauth2/access_token -d "grant_type=client_credentials"
```
Or:
```
  $ curl -X POST https://xyzfinancial.unqork.io/api/1.0/oauth2/access_token -d "grant_type=password&username={username}&password={password}"
```
This returns an "access_token", which you would then retain and use in any subsequent resource requests. **Access tokens expire after one hour, at which point you must retrieve a new one.** The access token should be included in a request header:
```
  $ curl -H "Authorization: Bearer {access_token}" https://xyzfinancial.unqork.io/api/1.0/{endpoint}
```
